title: Modelling & Applied Computing
outline: The aim of this course is to introduce Applied Mathematics and Mathematical Modelling through approximations and estimation theory, dimensional analysis, numerical methods, rudimentary modelling using first- and second-order ordinary differential equations, discrete- and continuous-time dynamical systems, and modelling and simulation of discrete- and continuous-time processes with MATLAB.
lecture_times: First Semester: 2nd period Monday, Wednesday, Friday. Second Semester: 2nd period Tuesday, Thursday.
dp_requirements: A class record of 30% or more; satisfactory attendance at lectures and tutorials; submission of all assigned work.
assessment: Class record counts 20–40%; one 3-hour examination written in October/November makes up the balance.
